channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A woman is rescued from rubble in Myanmar but hope is fading for more quake survivors
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue workers saved a 63-year-old woman from the rubble of a building in Myanmar's capital Tuesday, but hope was fading of finding many more survivors of the violent earthquake that killed at least 2,000, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a bloody civil war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A 17-year-old from the West Bank becomes the first Palestinian teenager to die in an Israeli prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A 17-year-old from the West Bank who was held in an Israeli prison for six months without being charged died after collapsing in unclear circumstances, becoming the first Palestinian teen to die in Israeli detention, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's Ishiba says he will push for US auto tariffs exemption
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba said Tuesday his government is making last ditch efforts to get U.S. President Donald Trump to exclude his country from an increase in auto tariffs due to take effect from Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the ruling barring Marine Le Pen from seeking public office for five years
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has been sentenced to a five-year ban on running for public office for embezzling EU funds, a major political earthquake. The ruling, which takes effect immediately, is likely to bar her from running in France’s next presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel strikes building in southern Beirut, killing at least 3 people
item.description: The Israeli military says it struck a building in Beirut's southern suburbs, killing at least three people.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela will have its first female saint after approval from Pope Francis
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela is set to have its first female saint following approval from Pope Francis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin tests limits of Trump’s patience in ceasefire talks
item.description: President Trump is expressing increased frustration with Russian President Vladimir Putin, whose shifting demands in negotiations with Ukraine have drawn out ceasefire talks and set back Trump's hopes of a quick end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US sanctions Sinaloa Cartel associates for alleged money laundering
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States Treasury sanctioned six people and seven companies Monday for alleged money laundering for factions of Mexico's Sinaloa Cartel, underscoring the group's new designation as a foreign terrorist organization under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin open to contacts after Trump criticism: Kremlin
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says he's open to staying in touch with the White House after critical comments from the U.S. president toward Russia's leader, which have been rare amid his efforts to get peace talks rolling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Iran's Khamenei warns of 'strong blow' as Trump threatens to drop bombs, Putin silent on US ire
item.description: Iran and Russia took decisively different approaches in response to President Donald Trump's threats as Tehran vowed action against any "mischief" while the Kremlin remains tight-lipped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Klobuchar: ‘Israel was incredibly angry’ about Signal leak
item.description: Sen. Amy Klobuchar (D-Minn.) said Israel was “incredibly angry” about the plans to attack Houthi rebels in Yemen leaked in a Signal group chat with Trump administration officials and a journalist. Klobuchar joined MSNBC’s Jen Psaki on Sunday, where she weighed in on the ongoing scandal.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump says ‘terrorists who attacked the beautiful Turnberry’ have been captured
item.description: President Trump said he was informed that the “terrorists who attacked" his Turnberry golf resort in Scotland had been captured.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Echoing Hamas, Erdogan reportedly calls for Israel's destruction amid push to rekindle ties with Trump
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan reportedly called for Israel's destruction during Eid prayers, drawing fierce condemnation from Israel, amid rising regional tensions over Gaza and Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces order evacuation for most of Rafah ahead of attack on the area
item.description: The Israeli military issued evacuation orders on Monday for most of Rafah and suggested it may soon launch another ground operation in the city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Danish leader: 'Nothing is stopping the Americans' from having more bases in Greenland
item.description: Former Danish Prime Minister Helle Thorning-Schmidt said there is nothing that’s stopping the Americans from setting up more bases in Greenland amid the territory’s fight to not be acquired by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope's willingness to show his frailty provides an example to young and old alike in aging world
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ frailty was on full view as he left Rome’s Gemelli hospital last Sunday after five weeks battling pneumonia that nearly killed him. He could barely lift his arms to bless the crowd. His eyes were sunken, face bloated.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medical supplies in great need as international assistance flows into Myanmar after earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Emergency aid has streamed into Southeast Asia in the two days since a massive earthquake struck Myanmar and Thailand. Relief efforts are focused on Myanmar, where the estimated death toll rose to 1,644 by Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey arrests Swedish journalist over alleged terrorist connections to a pro-Kurdish group
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish authorities said Sunday they arrested a Swedish journalist dispatched to cover ongoing nationwide protests on charges of terrorism and insulting the president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK bans ninja swords in move to crack down on violent knife crime
item.description: As knife crime continues to plague the U.K., the Labour government is attempting to crack down by adding ninja swords to the list of banned knives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump effect forces Germany to reprioritize defense as nation plays catch-up in military spending
item.description: In his second term as president, Donald Trump is pushing American allies, including Germany, to do more to fund their militaries and pay their fair share to fund defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose "male" or "female" on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation
